Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Circuit-level PDP-11/34 emulator 27 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. I Built an Open-World Engine for the N64 [video] 268 upvotes, 37 comments
  3. StationeryObject 28 upvotes, 1 comments
  4. Undroidwish – a single-file, batteries-included Tcl/Tk binary for many platforms 26 upvotes, 1 comments
  5. rpg.actor Game Jam 40 upvotes, 3 comments
  6. Linux is an interpreter 92 upvotes, 13 comments
  7. Paper Tape Is All You Need – Training a Transformer on a 1976 Minicomputer 103 upvotes, 17 comments
  8. Detecting file changes on macOS with kqueue 20 upvotes, 1 comments
  9. Founder of GitLab battles cancer by founding companies 269 upvotes, 65 comments
  10. Further human + AI + proof assistant work on Knuth's "Claude Cycles" problem 50 upvotes, 8 comments
  11. Spanish legislation as a Git repo 623 upvotes, 189 comments
  12. Arm releases first in-house chip, with Meta as debut customer 88 upvotes, 24 comments
  13. Cocoa-Way – Native macOS Wayland compositor for running Linux apps seamlessly 251 upvotes, 85 comments
  14. The bee that everyone wants to save 232 upvotes, 78 comments
  15. I decompiled the White House's new app 220 upvotes, 74 comments
  16. Anatomy of the .claude/ folder 576 upvotes, 245 comments
  17. Improved Git Diffs with Delta, Fzf and a Little Shell Scripting 81 upvotes, 28 comments
  18. CERN uses ultra-compact AI models on FPGAs for real-time LHC data filtering 259 upvotes, 122 comments
  19. Go hard on agents, not on your filesystem 539 upvotes, 300 comments
  20. AMD's Ryzen 9 9950X3D2 Dual Edition crams 208MB of cache into a single chip 273 upvotes, 149 comments
  21. Improving personal tax filing with Claude CLI and Obsidian 13 upvotes, 3 comments
  22. Make macOS consistently bad unironically 496 upvotes, 337 comments
  23. Go Naming Conventions: A Practical Guide 80 upvotes, 49 comments
  24. Folk are getting dangerously attached to AI that always tells them they're right 218 upvotes, 166 comments
  25. AI overly affirms users asking for personal advice 406 upvotes, 330 comments
  26. C++26: A User-Friednly assert() macro 47 upvotes, 32 comments
  27. RSA and Python 15 upvotes, 10 comments
  28. Nothing new to see here 7 upvotes, 3 comments
  29. Nobody Reads Your Setup Docs 6 upvotes, 7 comments
  30. Toma (YC W24) is hiring a Senior/Staff Eng to build AI automotive coworkers 0 upvotes, 0 comments
